title: taraxacum officinale subsp. dens-leonis
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. dens-leonis, commonly known as the Lion's Tooth or Dandelion Lion's Tooth, is a subspecies of the dandelion plant (Taraxacum officinale) that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Taraxacum officinale subsp. dens-leonis include its ability to support liver and kidney function, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as constipation and bloating. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to promote overall health and well-being, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
